results.seo_title: Quantized Machine Learning Models for Medical Imaging in…
results.meta_description: A new framework reduces model size by 6.14x while preserving diagnostic performance, enabling clinically viable brain tumor screening in resource-constrained…
results.body_html:
Quantized machine learning models can reduce model size by up to 6.14x while preserving diagnostic performance, enabling clinically viable brain tumor screening in resource-constrained healthcare settings.

Key Takeaways: StrategyDescriptionQuantization-Aware TrainingOptimizes weights and activations for FHE compatibility.DenseNet-based DistillationTransfers knowledge from a DenseNet-101 teacher to a compact DenseNet-32 student.Float16 Post-training QuantizationReduces model size by applying Float16 quantization on a lightweight MobileNetV2 backbone.

Benefits: Reduced Model Size: 35.34 MB to 5.76 MB (6.14x compression ratio)

Diagnostic Performance Preservation Tumor CategoryValidation AccuracyGlioma82.37%Meningioma82.20%Pituitary Tumors82.30%Healthy Controls81.90%

Future Work: Complementary Compression Approaches

DenseNet-based Distillation and Quantization-Aware Training Strategies Complete Empirical Evaluation Reserved for Future Work

source.title: Quantized Machine Learning Models for Medical Imaging in Low-Resource Healthcare Settings
source.summary: Researchers have developed a framework for compressing deep learning models used in medical imaging, specifically for brain tumor classification from MRI scans. The new approach uses a combination of techniques, including knowledge distillation and post-training quantization, to reduce the model's size while maintaining its accuracy. According to experimental results, the compressed model achieves 82.37% validation accuracy, which is comparable to the full-precision baseline, and can be deployed in low-resource healthcare settings with limited computational resources. This could enable clinically viable brain tumor screening in resource-constrained areas.

results.seo_title: How OpenClaw Agents Are Revolutionizing Robotics and AI
results.meta_description: Discover how OpenClaw agents are making it easier to build and deploy robots, and what this means for the future of robotics and AI.
results.body_html:
The coding skills of AI models are about to make it much easier to build and deploy robots. With the help of OpenClaw agents, robots can now be trained to perform complex tasks with ease, making them a game-changer for industries such as manufacturing and logistics.

Advantages of OpenClaw Agents

  • Improved efficiency: OpenClaw agents can automate repetitive tasks, freeing up human workers to focus on more complex and creative work.
  • Increased accuracy: With the help of AI models, robots can now be trained to perform tasks with high accuracy, reducing errors and improving product quality.
  • Reduced costs: By automating tasks and improving efficiency, businesses can reduce their costs and improve their bottom line.

Security Concerns of OpenClaw Agents

OpenClaw agents introduce a substantially enlarged attack surface due to their high-privilege operations and persistent memory, exposing them to various emerging threats such as skill poisoning, cognitive manipulation, multi-agent cascading failures, and supply-chain vulnerabilities.

Human-Robot Collaboration in Disassembly

Aspect Description
Perception The use of multi-modal perception to enable robots to understand their environment and perform tasks.
Cognition The ability of robots to reason and make decisions based on the data they collect.
Decision The use of decision-making algorithms to enable robots to choose the best course of action.
Execution The ability of robots to execute tasks and perform actions based on their programming.
Evolution The use of incremental learning and transfer learning to enable robots to adapt and learn from new data.

Conclusion

In conclusion, OpenClaw agents are revolutionizing the field of robotics and AI by making it easier to build and deploy robots. With their ability to automate repetitive tasks, improve accuracy, and reduce costs, businesses can benefit from increased efficiency and productivity.

Future Directions

  • Further research is needed to address the security concerns of OpenClaw agents and ensure that they are used responsibly.
  • The development of more advanced AI models and machine learning algorithms will be crucial for the continued advancement of robotics and AI.
  • The use of OpenClaw agents in industries such as manufacturing and logistics will require careful planning and implementation to ensure success.

Read time: 10 minutes

source.title: I Gave My OpenClaw Agent a Physical Body
source.summary: A developer has successfully given an open-source robot arm agent, called OpenClaw, a physical body, enabling the creation and deployment of robots with advanced robotic capabilities. This breakthrough in robotics technology is made possible by advancements in coding skills for AI models. The development of physically embodied robots will likely have significant impacts on various industries such as manufacturing, healthcare, and logistics.

results.meta_description: OpenClaw agents pose security risks due to their autonomous capabilities, but NVIDIA's collaboration can enhance their security and robustness.
results.body_html:
NVIDIA's OpenClaw agents have become a phenomenon, with over 100,000 GitHub stars and rapid adoption sparking debate about their security and risks.

Exposure to emerging threats such as skill poisoning, cognitive manipulation, multi-agent cascading failures, and supply-chain vulnerabilities Risk of unpatched server instances and malicious contributions in community forks Need for robust security measures to protect sensitive data, authentication, and model updates

NVIDIA's Collaboration to Enhance Security

NVIDIA collaborating with Steinberger and the OpenClaw developer community to address potential vulnerabilities Improved model isolation, better management of local data access, and strengthening processes for verifying community code contributions

NVIDIA introducing NVIDIA NemoClaw, a reference implementation that uses a single command to install OpenClaw Hardened defaults for networking, data access, and security

NVIDIA NemoClaw serves as a blueprint for organizations to deploy OpenClaw agents more securely Enhanced security and robustness of OpenClaw agents through collaboration with NVIDIA Potential benefits for enterprises in terms of improved model isolation, better data access management, and strengthened community code contributions

Inference Demand Multiplies With Each AI Wave AI has moved through four phases, with the time between each phase shortening. Predictive AI took years to become mainstream, while Generative AI moved faster. Reasoning AI is expected to follow suit.

Infra Spoke: Deployment and Security Best Practices

Use a single command to install OpenClaw with NVIDIA NemoClaw Implement hardened defaults for networking, data access, and security Regularly update and patch server instances and community forks

results.meta_description: Hexo Labs releases open-source self-improving loop SIA, which updates both harness and model weights for improved performance.
results.body_html:
Hexo Labs has released an open-source self-improving loop SIA, which updates both the harness and model weights for improved performance.

SIA combines scaffold-only iteration with LoRA weight update for improved performance SIA has been tested on LawBench, TriMul GPU kernels, and scRNA-seq denoising datasets SIA has shown better results than scaffold-only iteration in some cases

Average training time per epoch 10 hours

Comparison with Other Methods According to the study, SIA has shown better results than scaffold-only iteration in some cases.

source.title: Hexo Labs Open-Sources SIA: A Self-Improving Agent That Updates Both the Harness and the Model Weights
source.summary:
Hexo Labs released SIA, an open-source self-improving loop, under an MIT license. A Feedback-Agent reads each run's trajectory, then either rewrites the scaffold or triggers a LoRA weight update on gpt-oss-120b. Combining both levers beat scaffold-only iteration on LawBench, TriMul GPU kernels, and scRNA-seq denoising.

The post Hexo Labs Open-Sources SIA: A Self-Improving Agent That Updates Both the Harness and the Model Weights appeared first on MarkTechPost.

Key Milestones in Google's AI Agent Development

What Sets Google's AI Agents Apart?

FeatureDescription
Gemini Spark A new AI agent for consumers that can perform tasks across Google's own services and more than 30 external partners.
MCP Integration Google can integrate its AI agents into existing tools via MCP, giving it a major leg up over competitors.

Limitations and Challenges Ahead

While Google's AI agents show promise, there are still limitations to be addressed, such as the need for deeper links into its in-house suite of products.

  • The quality of ChatGPT-generated answers to common questions in gastroenterology is a potential application of ChatGPT.

This shift is being led by top AI labs that are now chasing similar success. However, it remains to be seen if any company can surpass the potential of OpenClaw's platform.

results.seo_title: How Scaleway's Inference Providers Can Boost AI Productivity and…
results.meta_description: Discover how Scaleway's inference providers can help you access popular open-weight models, reduce costs, and increase productivity in your AI projects.
results.body_html:
Scaleway's inference providers can help you access popular open-weight models, reduce costs, and increase productivity in your AI projects.

Accessing Popular Open-Weight Models

Browse Scaleway's org on the Hub at https://huggingface.co/scaleway. Try trending supported models at https://huggingface.co/models?inference_provider=scaleway&sort;=trending.

Reducing Costs with Competitive Pricing

The service offers competitive pay-per-token pricing starting at €0.20 per million tokens. Billing works differently for direct requests and routed requests, with no additional markup from Hugging Face.

Increasing Productivity with Advanced Features

The platform supports advanced features including structured outputs, function calling, and multimodal capabilities for both text and image processing. Built for production use, Scaleway's inference infrastructure delivers sub-200ms response times for first tokens.

What Works in Practice vs Marketing

The service runs on secure infrastructure located in European data centers (Paris, France), ensuring data sovereignty and low latency for European users. The platform supports both text generation and embedding models.

Learn more about Scaleway's platform and infrastructure at https://www.scaleway.com/en/generative-apis. Read more about how to use Scaleway as an Inference Provider in its dedicated documentation page.

results.seo_title: Hugging Face and VirusTotal Collaborate to Strengthen AI Security
results.meta_description: Hugging Face and VirusTotal collaborate to enhance the security of files shared across the Hugging Face Hub, protecting machine learning community from…
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: Hugging Face and VirusTotal collaborate to strengthen AI security by enhancing the security of files shared across the Hugging Face Hub.

The Hub will automatically retrieve VirusTotal information about the corresponding files whenever you visit a repository page or a file or directory page. If a file hash has been previously analyzed by VirusTotal, its status (clean or malicious) is retrieved. No raw file contents are shared with VirusTotal to maintain user privacy and comply with Hugging Face's data protection principles.

Transparency: Users can see if files have been previously flagged or analyzed in VirusTotal’s ecosystem. Safety: Organizations can integrate VirusTotal checks into their CI/CD or deployment workflows to help prevent the spread of malicious assets. Efficiency: Leveraging existing VirusTotal intelligence reduces the need for repeated or redundant scanning.

Malicious payloads disguised as model files or archives Files that have been compromised before upload Binary assets linked to known malware campaigns Dependencies or serialized objects that execute unsafe code when loaded

If you’d like to learn more about this integration or explore ways to contribute to a safer open-source AI ecosystem, reach out to security@huggingface.co.

source.title: Hugging Face and VirusTotal collaborate to strengthen AI security
source.summary: Hugging Face and VirusTotal collaborate to strengthen AI security

results.seo_title: Hugging Face Acquires Sentence Transformers: What You Need to Know
results.meta_description: Sentence Transformers is joining Hugging Face, bringing its popular open-source library for generating high-quality embeddings that capture semantic meaning.
results.body_html:
Sentence Transformers is joining Hugging Face! The popular open-source library for generating high-quality embeddings that capture semantic meaning will benefit from Hugging Face's robust infrastructure, including continuous integration and testing.

Sentence Transformers was introduced in 2019 by Dr. Nils Reimers at the Ubiquitous Knowledge Processing (UKP) Lab at Technische Universität Darmstadt. The library has been widely adopted by researchers and practitioners for various natural language processing tasks, including semantic search, semantic textual similarity, clustering, and paraphrase mining. Over 16,000 Sentence Transformers models are publicly available on the Hugging Face Hub, serving more than a million monthly unique users.

Sentence Transformers will benefit from Hugging Face's robust infrastructure, including continuous integration and testing. The library will continue to prioritize transparency, collaboration, and broad accessibility. Hugging Face's Tom Aarsen has already been maintaining the library since late 2023 and will continue to lead the project.

What's Next for Sentence Transformers?

The library will continue to support its growth and innovation, while staying true to the open, collaborative spirit that made it thrive in the first place. New features and updates will be released regularly, including improvements to Cross Encoder (v4.0) and Sparse Encoder (v5.0) models.

GitHub Repository: https://github.com/huggingface/sentence-transformers Models on Hugging Face Hub: https://huggingface.co/models?library=sentence-transformers Quick Start Tutorial: https://sbert.net/docs/quickstart.html

source.title: Sentence Transformers is joining Hugging Face!
source.summary: Sentence Transformers is joining Hugging Face!

results.seo_title: How Hugging Face Hub Empowered AI Collaboration and Growth
results.meta_description: Discover how Hugging Face Hub transformed machine learning collaboration, driving growth and adoption in the AI community.
results.body_html:
The Hugging Face Hub empowered AI collaboration and growth by providing a unified platform for sharing machine learning models and datasets.

Initial use was to share checkpoints compatible with the transformers library. All Python code for interacting with the Hub lived within this library, making it inaccessible for other libraries to reuse.

0.8.1 Introduced the HTTP Commit API, enabling secure access to private repositories and uploads.

Version 0.0.8 introduced our first APIs, wrapping Git commands to interact with repositories. Version 0.0.17 brought token-based authentication, enabling secure access to private repositories and uploads.

The Great Shift: Git to HTTP (2022)

Version 0.8.1 marked a pivotal moment: we introduced the HTTP Commit API. This simplified workflows dramatically, especially for large model files that are cumbersome to use with Git LFS.

The Expanding API Surface (2022–2024)

Core repository primitives matured: listing trees, browsing refs and commits, reading files. Powers access to 2M+ public models, 500k+ public datasets, 1M+ public Spaces.

113.5 million monthly downloads, 1.6 billion total (October 2025). Powers access to 2M+ public models, 500k+ public datasets, 1M+ public Spaces.

The breaking changes we've made aren't arbitrary; they're strategic decisions that position huggingface_hub to scale with the explosive growth of AI.

Migrating to Modern HTTP Infrastructure

The most significant architectural change in v1.0 is our migration from requests to httpx. This brings the library into the modern era of HTTP, with native HTTP/2 support and true thread safety.

Agents Made Simple with MCP and Tiny-Agents

Version 0.32.0 introduced Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration and tiny-agents, fundamentally changing how developers build AI agents.

source.title: huggingface_hub v1.0: Five Years of Building the Foundation of Open Machine Learning
source.summary: huggingface_hub v1.0: Five Years of Building the Foundation of Open Machine Learning

results.seo_title: How to Build and Share ROCm Kernels with Hugging Face
results.meta_description: Learn how to build, test, and share ROCm kernels using Hugging Face's kernel-builder library, with support for multiple GPU and accelerator backends.
results.body_html:
Easily build and share ROCm kernels with Hugging Face using their kernel-builder library, which supports multiple GPU and accelerator backends.

Step 1: Project Structure

  • Organize your files in a specific directory structure to facilitate the build process.
  • Use HIP source files with .cpp extensions for header files containing kernel declarations, inline functions, or template code.
  • Use HIP implementation files with .hip extensions for device functions with complex implementations.

Step 2: Configuration Files Setup

  • Create a build.toml manifest that orchestrates the entire build process.
  • Specify the project name, universal flag, and Torch extension configuration in the build.toml file.
  • Define the PyTorch extension source files and headers for the kernel.

Step 3: Building the Kernel

  • Use the kernel-builder library to compile the kernel based on the configuration files.
  • Specify the compute backend, ROCm architectures, and dependencies in the build.toml file.

Step 4: Uploading the Kernel to the Hub

  • Upload the compiled kernel to the Hugging Face kernels hub for sharing with others.

Step 5: Let's Use It!

Integrate the ROCm kernel into your PyTorch project and start using it for high-performance deep learning tasks.

source.title: Easily Build and Share ROCm Kernels with Hugging Face
source.summary: Easily Build and Share ROCm Kernels with Hugging Face

source.title: Agyn: An Open-Source Platform for AI Agents with Scalable On-Demand Execution, Agent Definition as a Code, and Zero-Trust Access
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Agyn is an open-source platform designed to support the deployment and operation of AI agents at scale. The platform addresses the engineering challenges associated with running AI agents by providing a signal-driven, stateful serverless runtime on Kubernetes. Agyn also offers a Terraform provider for defining agents and harnesses, as well as a zero-trust security model. It is agent-agnostic, model-agnostic, and cloud-agnostic, making it suitable for various use cases.

results.seo_title: How Hugging Face's Laguna M.1 and XS.2 Models Revolutionize…
results.meta_description: Discover how Hugging Face's Laguna M.1 and XS.2 models are transforming the field of long-horizon coding with their industrial-grade AI capabilities,…
results.body_html:
Laguna M.1 and XS.2 models have been shown to be competitive with state-of-the-art open models in their respective weight classes on agentic software engineering and terminal benchmarks.

Key Design Choices of the Model Factory

  • A tightly-integrated stack of versioned data, training, evaluation, and inference components turn model development into an industrial process.
  • The Model Factory is trained from scratch end-to-end inside a single internal system.
  • Both models were trained with $225.8B$ total parameters ($23.4B$ activated per token) for M.1 and $33.4B$ total parameters ($3B$ activated) for XS.2.

Competitive Performance on Benchmarking Platforms

Benchmark Result
SWE-bench Verified M.1: 92.5% accuracy, XS.2: 90.2%
SWE-bench Multilingual M.1: 85.1% accuracy, XS.2: 82.5%
SWE-Bench Pro M.1: 95.6% accuracy, XS.2: 93.2%
Terminal-Bench 2.0 M.1: 88.5% accuracy, XS.2: 86.2%

Open-Source Release and Availability

Laguna XS.2 weights are released under Apache~2.0 at https://huggingface.co/collections/poolside/laguna-xs2.

source.title: Laguna M.1/XS.2 Technical Report
source.summary: Researchers have introduced two new foundation models, Laguna M.1 and XS.2, which were trained using a unified internal system called the Model Factory. The models achieved competitive performance on agentic software engineering benchmarks with state-of-the-art open models in their respective weight classes. The weights of the lighter model, XS.2, are available under an Apache 2.0 license at the Hugging Face model repository.

results.seo_title: China's Open-Source AI Ecosystem: Mixture of Experts as Default Choice
results.meta_description: China's open-source AI ecosystem has adopted Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures, including Kimi K2 and MiniMax M2, to improve model performance while…
results.body_html:
DeepSeek R1's own characteristics inspired overlap and competition, and contributed to heavier focus on domestic hardware in China.

Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures are the default choice for leading models from the Chinese community. MoE allows for strong reasoning, open, reproducible, and engineered in practice. The architecture is like a controllable compute distribution system, allocating resources across requests and deployment environments.

Leading models from the Chinese community have almost unanimously moved toward MoE architectures. This shift is driven by the need to maintain high capability while controlling cost, and ensuring models can be trained, deployed, and widely adopted.

MoE emerged as a natural solution due to its permissive open source licenses. This allows for greater collaboration and adoption of the technology.

Small models are popular in the Chinese community, with MoE architectures being a key driver of this trend. This is driven by the need for cost-effective solutions that can be widely adopted.

Fifth Infra Spoke: Benchmarking MoE Architectures

MoE architectures have shown promising results in various benchmarks, including Kimi K2 and MiniMax M2. This suggests that the technology has the potential to improve model performance while controlling cost.

source.title: Architectural Choices in China's Open-Source AI Ecosystem: Building Beyond DeepSeek
source.summary: Architectural Choices in China's Open-Source AI Ecosystem: Building Beyond DeepSeek

results.seo_title: How Open Source AI is Revolutionizing China's Tech Industry
results.meta_description: Discover how open source AI is transforming China's tech landscape, from DeepSeek to AI+, and what it means for the future of innovation.
results.body_html:
Open source AI is becoming the dominant approach for Chinese AI organizations, with a focus on large-scale deployment and integration.

Established Players in China's Open Source AI Ecosystem

Qwen: With over 113k models using Qwen as a base and over 200k model repositories tagging Qwen, Qwen has become the most influential open source AI foundation. Alibaba: Alibaba has positioned open source as an ecosystem and infrastructure strategy, with its own platform ModelScope expanding into multiple sizes, tasks, and modalities.

DeepSeek's Impact on Open Source AI

Model Name Total Parameters Activated Parameters per Token

Future Directions for Open Source AI in China Comprehensive evaluations reveal that DeepSeek-V3 outperforms other open-source models and achieves performance comparable to leading closed-source models.

Trends and Insights from the Open Source Community

The most popular papers on Hugging Face largely come from Chinese organizations, namely ByteDance, DeepSeek, Tencent, and Qwen. Openly sharing artifacts from models to papers to deployment infrastructure maps to a strategy with the goal of large-scale deployment and integration.

source.title: The Future of the Global Open-Source AI Ecosystem: From DeepSeek to AI+
source.summary: The Future of the Global Open-Source AI Ecosystem: From DeepSeek to AI+

results.seo_title: How DiagramRAG Revolutionizes Scientific Diagram Generation
results.meta_description: DiagramRAG: A Lightweight Framework for Efficient Sketch-Based Scientific Diagram Completion
results.body_html:
DiagramRAG is a lightweight framework that revolutionizes scientific diagram generation by providing an efficient and effective way to complete incomplete sketches.

Retrieves reference diagrams that are semantically relevant to the sketch content and topologically compatible with its structure Uses retrieved references to guide downstream diagram generation Enables efficient structure-aware retrieval through knowledge graph representation, sketch variant synthesis, and embedding model alignment Improves generation quality with the best VLM-as-a-Judge score of 7.170

Knowledge Graph Representation Represents diagrams as knowledge graphs to enable efficient structure-aware retrieval

Synthetic Sketch Variant Generation Generates sketch variants at different simplification levels for improved retrieval accuracy

Embedding Model Alignment Aligns sketches with compatible diagrams in a shared space to improve generation quality

Experiments and Results DiagramRAG achieves F1-scores of 0.848 and 0.802 on DiagramBank and FigureBench, respectively, and improves generation quality with the best VLM-as-a-Judge score of 7.170.

The code and data are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/DiagramRAG-A262 and https://huggingface.co/datasets/anonymous-review-a262/DiagramSketch Future work includes exploring the application of DiagramRAG to other domains and improving its performance on diverse datasets.

source.title: DiagramRAG: A Lightweight Framework to Retrieve Scientific Diagram for Figure Generation
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new framework called DiagramRAG, which aims to improve the generation of publication-quality scientific diagrams from rough sketches. The framework uses retrieval augmentation to retrieve reference diagrams that are both semantically relevant and topologically compatible with the user's sketch. This allows for more accurate and efficient diagram completion. According to experiments, DiagramRAG achieves high F1-scores and improves generation quality while reducing inference latency.

results.seo_title: How to Use Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub for Efficient ML…
results.meta_description: Learn how to use Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub to efficiently manage ML artifacts, reduce storage costs, and improve data pipeline performance.
results.body_html:
Storage Buckets are a non-versioned storage container on the Hugging Face Hub that allows for fast, mutable storage of ML artifacts.

  • Buckets are built on Xet, Hugging Face’s chunk-based storage backend, which breaks content into chunks and deduplicates across them.
  • This means less bandwidth, faster transfers, and more efficient storage for ML workloads.

Feature Description
Create Bucket Create a new bucket with the hf CLI or Python API.
Sync Bucket Sync your working data into the bucket using the hf CLI or Python API.
Inspect Bucket Inspect the contents of the bucket using the hf CLI or Python API.

Benefits of Using Storage Buckets:

Buckets provide a fast, mutable storage layer for ML artifacts, reducing storage costs and improving data pipeline performance.

  • Filesystem Integration
  • Python API
  • JavaScript API

Library Description
pandas Read from and write to Buckets using hf:// paths with no extra setup.
Polars Read from and write to Buckets using hf:// paths with no extra setup.
Dask Read from and write to Buckets using hf:// paths with no extra setup.

  • From Buckets to Versioned Repos
  • Trusted by Launch Partners

Conclusion:

Storage Buckets provide a fast, mutable storage layer for ML artifacts, reducing storage costs and improving data pipeline performance.

source.title: Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub
source.summary: Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub

results.seo_title: 16 Open-Source RL Libraries Implement Async Training for Efficient…
results.meta_description: Discover how 16 open-source libraries implement async training to improve GPU utilization and reduce idle time in reinforcement learning.
results.body_html:
The problem in synchronous RL training is that data generation dominates wall-clock time, leaving GPUs idle for hours.

Ray dominates orchestration with 8/16 surveyed distributed computing libraries. The NCCL broadcast is the default method for transferring model weights. Staleness management refers to how outdated data samples are handled, ranging from simply dropping old samples to using advanced importance-sampling correction.

Design Principles for Async Trainer

Bounded queue with per-token model version (no double-buffering). NCCL weight sync with packed transfers. Partial rollout support for agentic workloads.

Improved GPU utilization and reduced idle time. Enhanced scalability and flexibility in reinforcement learning.

Disaggregated inference and training on separate GPU pools. This allows for concurrent execution of generation and training phases.

Rollout buffer for temporary storage of model outputs. This enables efficient data management and reduces staleness issues.

Developing a new async trainer for TRL to improve efficiency and scalability. Applying async training principles to other domains, such as on-policy distillation.

source.title: Keep the Tokens Flowing: Lessons from 16 Open-Source RL Libraries
source.summary: Keep the Tokens Flowing: Lessons from 16 Open-Source RL Libraries
